description.para: Register offsets for the XIic device.

sectiondef.header: Device Global Interrupt Enable Register masks (CR) mask(s)
IIC Device Interrupt Status/Enable (INTR) Register Masks

description.para: This register holds the interrupt status flags for the Spi device.

This register is used to enable interrupt sources for the IIC device. Writing a '1' to a bit in this register enables the corresponding Interrupt. Writing a '0' to a bit in this register disables the corresponding Interrupt.
IISR/IIER registers have the same bit definitions and are only defined once.

briefdescription.para: The following constants are used to specify whether to do Read or a Write operation on IIC bus.

detaileddescription.para: Read operation on the IIC bus

briefdescription.para: Write operation on the IIC bus.
The following constants are used with the transmit FIFO fill function to specify the role which the IIC device is acting as, a master or a slave.
The following constants are used with Transmit Function (XIic_Send) to specify whether to STOP after the current transfer of data or own the bus with a Repeated start.

detaileddescription.para: Send a stop on the IIC bus after the current data transfer

briefdescription.para: Do not Send a stop on the IIC bus after the current data transfer.
Read from the specified IIC device register.

parameterdescription.para: is the base address of the device.
is the offset from the 1st register of the device to select the specific register.

simplesect.para: The value read from the register.

C-Style signature: u32 XIic_ReadReg(u32 BaseAddress, u32 RegOffset); This macro does not do any checking to ensure that the register exists if the register may be excluded due to parameterization, such as the GPO Register.

briefdescription.para: Write to the specified IIC device register.

parameterdescription.para: is the base address of the device.
is the offset from the 1st register of the device to select the specific register.
is the value to be written to the register.

simplesect.para: C-Style signature: void XIic_WriteReg(u32 BaseAddress, u32 RegOffset, u32 RegisterValue); This macro does not do any checking to ensure that the register exists if the register may be excluded due to parameterization, such as the GPO Register.

briefdescription.para: This macro disables all interrupts for the device by writing to the Global interrupt enable register.

parameterdescription.para: is the base address of the IIC device.

briefdescription.para: This macro writes to the global interrupt enable register to enable interrupts from the device.

detaileddescription.para: This function does not enable individual interrupts as the Interrupt Enable Register must be set appropriately.

parameterdescription.para: is the base address of the IIC device.

briefdescription.para: This function determines if interrupts are enabled at the global level by reading the global interrupt register.

parameterdescription.para: is the base address of the IIC device.

listitem.para: TRUE if the global interrupt is enabled.
FALSE if global interrupt is disabled.

briefdescription.para: This function sets the Interrupt status register to the specified value.

detaileddescription.para: This register implements a toggle on write functionality. The interrupt is cleared by writing to this register with the bits to be cleared set to a one and all others to zero. Setting a bit which is zero within this register causes an interrupt to be generated.

This function writes only the specified value to the register such that some status bits may be set and others cleared. It is the caller's responsibility to get the value of the register prior to setting the value to prevent an destructive behavior.

parameterdescription.para: is the base address of the IIC device.
is the value to be written to the Interrupt status register.

simplesect.para: C-Style signature: void XIic_WriteIisr(u32 BaseAddress, u32 Status);

briefdescription.para: This function gets the contents of the Interrupt Status Register.

detaileddescription.para: This register indicates the status of interrupt sources for the device. The status is independent of whether interrupts are enabled such that the status register may also be polled when interrupts are not enabled.

parameterdescription.para: is the base address of the IIC device.

simplesect.para: The value read from the Interrupt Status Register.

briefdescription.para: This function sets the contents of the Interrupt Enable Register.

detaileddescription.para: This function writes only the specified value to the register such that some interrupt sources may be enabled and others disabled. It is the caller's responsibility to get the value of the interrupt enable register prior to setting the value to prevent a destructive behavior.

parameterdescription.para: is the base address of the IIC device.
is the value to be written to the Interrupt Enable Register. Bit positions of 1 will be enabled. Bit positions of 0 will be disabled.

simplesect.para: C-Style signature: void XIic_WriteIier(u32 BaseAddress, u32 Enable);

briefdescription.para: This function gets the Interrupt Enable Register contents.

parameterdescription.para: is the base address of the IIC device.

simplesect.para: The contents read from the Interrupt Enable Register. Bit positions of 1 indicate that the corresponding interrupt is enabled. Bit positions of 0 indicate that the corresponding interrupt is disabled.

briefdescription.para: This macro clears the specified interrupt in the Interrupt status register.

detaileddescription.para: It is non-destructive in that the register is read and only the interrupt specified is cleared. Clearing an interrupt acknowledges it.

parameterdescription.para: is the base address of the IIC device.
is the bit mask of the interrupts to be cleared.

simplesect.para: C-Style signature: void XIic_ClearIisr(u32 BaseAddress, u32 InterruptMask);

memberdef.name: XIic_Send7BitAddress
memberdef.initializer: { \ u8 LocalAddr = (u8)(SlaveAddress << 1); \ LocalAddr = (LocalAddr & 0xFE) | (Operation); \ XIic_WriteReg(BaseAddress, XIIC_DTR_REG_OFFSET, LocalAddr); \ }

briefdescription.para: This macro sends the address for a 7 bit address during both read and write operations.

detaileddescription.para: It takes care of the details to format the address correctly. This macro is designed to be called internally to the drivers.

parameterdescription.para: is the base address of the IIC Device.
is the address of the slave to send to.
indicates XIIC_READ_OPERATION or XIIC_WRITE_OPERATION

simplesect.para: C-Style signature: void XIic_Send7BitAddress(u32 BaseAddress, u8 SlaveAddress, u8 Operation);

memberdef.name: XIic_DynSend7BitAddress
memberdef.initializer: { \ u8 LocalAddr = (u8)(SlaveAddress << 1); \ LocalAddr = (LocalAddr & 0xFE) | (Operation); \ XIic_WriteReg(BaseAddress, XIIC_DTR_REG_OFFSET, \ XIIC_TX_DYN_START_MASK | LocalAddr); \ }

briefdescription.para: This macro sends the address for a 7 bit address during both read and write operations.

detaileddescription.para: It takes care of the details to format the address correctly. This macro is designed to be called internally to the drivers for Dynamic controller functionality.

parameterdescription.para: is the base address of the IIC Device.
is the address of the slave to send to.
indicates XIIC_READ_OPERATION or XIIC_WRITE_OPERATION.

simplesect.para: C-Style signature: void XIic_DynSend7BitAddress(u32 BaseAddress, u8 SlaveAddress, u8 Operation);

memberdef.name: XIic_DynSendStartStopAddress
memberdef.initializer: { \ u8 LocalAddr = (u8)(SlaveAddress << 1); \ LocalAddr = (LocalAddr & 0xFE) | (Operation); \ XIic_WriteReg(BaseAddress, XIIC_DTR_REG_OFFSET, \ XIIC_TX_DYN_START_MASK | XIIC_TX_DYN_STOP_MASK | \ LocalAddr); \ }

briefdescription.para: This macro sends the address, start and stop for a 7 bit address during both write operations.

detaileddescription.para: It takes care of the details to format the address correctly. This macro is designed to be called internally to the drivers.

parameterdescription.para: is the base address of the IIC Device.
is the address of the slave to send to.
indicates XIIC_WRITE_OPERATION.

simplesect.para: C-Style signature: void XIic_DynSendStartStopAddress(u32 BaseAddress, u8 SlaveAddress, u8 Operation);

briefdescription.para: This macro sends a stop condition on IIC bus for Dynamic logic.

parameterdescription.para: is the base address of the IIC Device.
is the number of Rx bytes received before the master. doesn't respond with ACK.

simplesect.para: C-Style signature: void XIic_DynSendStop(u32 BaseAddress, u32 ByteCount);

memberdef.argsstring: (UINTPTR BaseAddress, u8 Address, u8 *BufferPtr, unsigned ByteCount, u8 Option)
memberdef.name: XIic_Recv

briefdescription.para: Receive data as a master on the IIC bus.

detaileddescription.para: This function receives the data using polled I/O and blocks until the data has been received. It only supports 7 bit addressing mode of operation. This function returns zero if bus is busy.

parameterdescription.para: contains the base address of the IIC device.
contains the 7 bit IIC address of the device to send the specified data to.
points to the data to be sent.
is the number of bytes to be sent.
indicates whether to hold or free the bus after reception of data, XIIC_STOP = end with STOP condition, XIIC_REPEATED_START = don't end with STOP condition.

memberdef.argsstring: (UINTPTR BaseAddress, u8 Address, u8 *BufferPtr, unsigned ByteCount, u8 Option)
memberdef.name: XIic_Send

briefdescription.para: Send data as a master on the IIC bus.

detaileddescription.para: This function sends the data using polled I/O and blocks until the data has been sent. It only supports 7 bit addressing mode of operation. This function returns zero if bus is busy.

parameterdescription.para: contains the base address of the IIC device.
contains the 7 bit IIC address of the device to send the specified data to.
points to the data to be sent.
is the number of bytes to be sent.
indicates whether to hold or free the bus after transmitting the data.

memberdef.argsstring: (UINTPTR BaseAddress, u8 Address, u8 *BufferPtr, u8 ByteCount)
memberdef.name: XIic_DynRecv

briefdescription.para: Receive data as a master on the IIC bus.

detaileddescription.para: This function receives the data using polled I/O and blocks until the data has been received. It only supports 7 bit addressing. This function returns zero if bus is busy.

parameterdescription.para: contains the base address of the IIC Device.
contains the 7 bit IIC Device address of the device to send the specified data to.
points to the data to be sent.
is the number of bytes to be sent. This value can't be greater than 255 and needs to be greater than 0.

simplesect.para: Upon entry to this function, the IIC interface needs to be already enabled in the CR register.

memberdef.argsstring: (UINTPTR BaseAddress, u16 Address, u8 *BufferPtr, u8 ByteCount, u8 Option)
memberdef.name: XIic_DynSend

briefdescription.para: Send data as a master on the IIC bus.

detaileddescription.para: This function sends the data using polled I/O and blocks until the data has been sent. It only supports 7 bit addressing. This function returns zero if bus is busy.

parameterdescription.para: contains the base address of the IIC Device.
contains the 7 bit IIC address of the device to send the specified data to.
points to the data to be sent.
is the number of bytes to be sent.
XIIC_STOP = end with STOP condition, XIIC_REPEATED_START = don't end with STOP condition.

briefdescription.para: This function will wait until the I2C bus is free or timeout.

parameterdescription.para: contains the base address of the I2C device.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if the I2C bus was freed before the timeout.
